title: Sweet Irish Mackerel Stew
description: A sweet and gooey Irish stew featuring mackerel, perfect for a crowd. This jain-friendly dish is made with tender mackerel, fluffy dumplings, and a hint of sweetness.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add oil, onion, carrots, and potatoes to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 8-10 pieces and shape into dumplings.
In a large Dutch oven, combine mackerel fillets, apple cider vinegar, and maple syrup.
Add the dumplings to the pot and cover with a lid.
Bake for 25-30 minutes or until the mackerel is cooked through and the dumplings are tender.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or dill.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat gently over low heat, adding more liquid if needed.
